top.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
top.summary: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

top.title: Trump's paused attack on Iran's energy - for diplomacy or escalation?
top.summary: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

top.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
top.summary: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

top.title: Lloyds bank reveals IT glitch affected almost half a million customers
top.summary: In a letter to the Treasury Select Committee, Lloyds apologised and said some compensation had been paid.

top.title: Just Eat and Autotrader among firms investigated in fake reviews probe
top.summary: The UK's competition watchdog says it is looking at five firms in its investigation into misleading online reviews.

top.title: Judge rejects Pentagon's attempt to 'cripple' Anthropic
top.summary: A federal judge told the government it could not immediately enforce a ban on Anthropic’s tools.

title.#text: Meta’s court losses could be just the beginning
Sony is raising PS5 prices by $100 in April
